tool_args.label: MTA Open Data Law key provisions
tool_args.raw_text: Designate a data coordinator, who is responsible for managing the program and complying with the law. Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data. Define a portal to contribute to, which in our case, was New York State's open data portal at data.ny.gov.

payload.claim_text: The MTA's open data law contains language that is common across many open data policies and statutes. A few pieces of the legislation were particularly helpful: Designate a data coordinator; Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data; Define a portal to contribute to (data.ny.gov).
payload.description: Key provisions that helped establish the MTA Open Data Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: Designate a data coordinator... Create a three-year plan... Define a portal to contribute to

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: MTA publishes annual open data program update
tool_args.raw_text: While not included in our statutory requirements, we still publish an annual update on our program page

payload.claim_text: While not included in our statutory requirements, we still publish an annual update on our program page, which we have found incredibly valuable in memorializing our success.
payload.description: MTA publishes annual open data program updates voluntarily

evidence_refs.source_quote: While not included in our statutory requirements, we still publish an annual update on our program page

tool_args.label: metrics.mta.info visualization tool
tool_args.raw_text: We put quite a bit of work into building our data visualization tool metrics.mta.info early on

payload.claim_text: We put quite a bit of work into building our data visualization tool metrics.mta.info early on, which visualizes open datasets in a way that is easily digestible for people of all technical skill levels.
payload.description: MTA built metrics.mta.info as a data visualization tool for open data

evidence_refs.source_quote: We put quite a bit of work into building our data visualization tool metrics.mta.info early on

tool_args.label: MTA Open Data Law key provisions
tool_args.raw_text: A few pieces of the legislation were particularly helpful in establishing how the program would work: Designate a data coordinator, who is responsible for managing the program and complying with the law. Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data. Define a portal to contribute to, which in our case, was New York State's open data portal at data.ny.gov.

payload.claim_text: The MTA's open data law requires: Designate a data coordinator; Create a three-year plan to release all publishable data; Define a portal to contribute to (data.ny.gov).
payload.description: Key provisions that helped establish the MTA Open Data Program

tool_args.label: Open data team contact
tool_args.raw_text: A team inbox (opendata@mtahq.org) has worked well for us.

payload.claim_text: A team inbox (opendata@mtahq.org) has worked well for us.
payload.description: Open data program team email contact

tool_args.label: Open Data Program key player roles
tool_args.raw_text: Key players Identifying who needs to be part of the immediate team... Program manager... Data scientist(s)... Data engineer(s)... Internal leadership... Legal serves as an important safeguard... Internal users... External users and advocates...

payload.claim_text: Key players for starting an open data program: Program manager (day-to-day operations, liaison), Data scientists (write code to produce datasets), Data engineers (architect automation tooling), Internal leadership (executive sponsorship), Legal and Communications leadership, Internal users (cross-departmental data sharing), External users and advocates.
payload.description: Seven key contributor roles identified for a successful open data program

tool_args.label: First datasets release strategy
tool_args.raw_text: When identifying and preparing your first datasets for release, we recommend prioritizing easy wins, such as data that is already made public (but not open) in some form or data that is frequently requested through the FOIL process.

payload.claim_text: When identifying and preparing first datasets for release, prioritize easy wins: data already made public in some form or data frequently FOILed.
payload.description: Strategy to focus on data already public (e.g. PDFs) or frequently FOILed to avoid needing formalized data governance policy first

tool_args.label: Manual uploads in first year
tool_args.raw_text: After our first year of the program, we were still manually uploading every single open dataset to the open data portal. We desperately needed the expertise of a data engineer to scale the program in a sustainable way.

payload.claim_text: After our first year of the program, we were still manually uploading every single open dataset to the open data portal. We desperately needed the expertise of a data engineer to scale the program.
payload.description: First year of program used manual uploads; data engineer expertise was needed for scaling

tool_args.label: Open Data Program enabled by Open Data Law
tool_args.raw_text: this mandate was the catalyst for establishing a formal program and freeing up the resources needed to get the program off the ground.

payload.description: The MTA Open Data Law was the catalyst for establishing the formal open data program
